events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Field service high voltage safety. Observed condition: glove class wrong or test date expired (ID from stamp). Work actively in progress. Need identify hazards severity immediate controls. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high or critical? HV gloves wrong class or expired test date means risk of electric shock / arc flash, potentially fatal. Severity critical? In NFPA 70E, using uninsulated or untested gloves on HV is high risk. Since actively in progress, immediate exposure. Risk likely critical. component_conditions: installed-defective? glove class wrong or test date expired is non-compliant, maybe worn/degraded? The categories allowed: installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Expired test date = non-compliant; wrong class = non-compliant / installed-defective? Use non-compliant. Could include degraded? Not physical degradation. We'll use ["non-compliant"] maybe also "installed-defective" if glove is installed as PPE but defective? The scenario says glove class wrong or test date expired (ID from stamp). That is non-compliant. Could include installed-defective because PPE is defective as installed? But component condition categories may apply: installed-defective, non-compliant. Use both maybe. Need percent_complete estimate. Work in progress, maybe 40%. value not asked 0. sound_source etc default 0/empty. workable empty. findings: list short defect/hazard. actions immediate corrective. rationale. references: NFPA 70E, OEM HV procedures. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "HV insulating gloves are the wrong class for the system voltage or have an expired test date per the stamp",
    "PPE is not verified as electrically safe before live or potentially energized HV work",
    "Worker is actively exposed to high-voltage shock/arc-flash hazard with inadequate hand protection"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and remove personnel from the HV hazard zone",
    "De-energize, isolate, and verify zero energy state per OEM HV lockout/tagout procedure before continuing",
    "Remove the non-compliant gloves from service and replace with correctly class-rated, in-date, factory-tested insulating gloves",
    "Inspect and test all HV PPE; re-validate glove test dates and voltage ratings against the asset's maximum working voltage",
    "Brief the crew on proper HV PPE selection and required pre-use inspection before resuming work"
  ],
  "rationale": "Using HV insulating gloves with the wrong voltage class or an expired test date violates NFPA 70E-adjacent PPE requirements and OEM HV safety procedures, creating a direct risk of fatal electric shock or arc flash during active service work. Work must be halted until properly rated, in-date gloves and verified de-energization are in place.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 70E-adjacent (electrical safety and PPE selection/use)",
    "OEM HV procedures (de-energization, LOTO, and insulating glove requirements)"
  ]
}
